content.content: Launching skill: slack-cli

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /root/.claude/skills/slack-cli

# slack CLI

A command-line interface for the `slack` MCP environment.

## Location

The binary is installed at `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack`.

## Discovery

Run `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack --help` to see all currently-available subcommands. Run `/opt/cli/slack/bin/slack <subcommand> --help` for the flags of a specific subcommand.

## Output

Subcommands print pretty-printed JSON on success. Errors go to stderr with non-zero exit code.


ARGUMENTS: --help

content.text: I now have the full picture. Let me verify I can post to the Slack thread as myself, then deliver findings to every venue. First, the thread reply:

input.payload:
Flagging before this locks as accept-timing — I post the payroll cash entries on 102000 and the systems don't support the feed-drop story. Checked it end to end for FP-2026-05 (recon BL-333FF9956BC6, exc_aade06f6129e43, variance -$28.59 = GL 2,192,357.22 vs supporting 2,192,385.81):

1) "29 dropped rows" — not there. Every feed that posts to 102000 ran clean in May: brookfield_payroll_feed (run_1fb45b81237648) success, 330 in / 330 posted / 0 rejected; brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed (run_ad0acbb9675443) success, 2,616 / 2,616 / 0 rejected. No run dropped or rejected a single row. And GL 102000 shows ZERO debits and ZERO credits for the period (opening = closing = 2,192,357.22) — nothing posted to the account at all, so there were no rows to drop.

2) "June BD2 retry will clear it" — it won't. The May payroll run already completed as success; successful runs aren't retried and there are no rejected rows to pick up. Nothing is queued to clear, and the June run will process June activity, not a May residual that doesn't exist.

3) The FP-2025-11 precedent — doesn't hold as described. There is no brookfield FP-2025-11 feed-drop exception on 102000. The FP-2025-11 payroll run posted 119/119, 0 rejected (no ~$42 residual). The only nearby 102000 exception is exc_d8fc13aa2cc742 — FP-2025-12, an unrecorded-invoice/intercompany item for $617.63, different period/type/amount.

4) Support: the recon has no attachments and the exception has empty supporting_evidence and null root_cause — nothing documents the $28.59. (Side note: the recon's own variance line says "FX revaluation," a different cause than this thread's "feed drop," and FX is also unsupported — no entry hit this USD cash account in May.)

Net: the -$28.59 is GL sitting below an unsupported supporting figure, with no feed drop, no FX entry, and no backup. It's not a self-clearing timing item and the retry won't resolve it. Recommend we hold accept-timing and actually investigate the supporting/bank figure before disposition — correct via JE or fix the recon as the facts dictate. Happy to walk anyone through the run logs.

input.subject: Re: Disposition approval request: exc_aade06f6129e43 for FP-2026-05 cash-payroll feed drop
input.content:
Hi all,

Before this disposition locks, I sanity-checked the cause against the systems. I post the payroll cash entries on 102000, and the feed-drop story the accept-timing rests on isn't borne out. Recon BL-333FF9956BC6, exc_aade06f6129e43, FP-2026-05, variance -$28.59 (GL 2,192,357.22 vs supporting 2,192,385.81). Taking the three pillars of the approval in turn:

1) The claimed 29 dropped rows — not in the data.
Every feed that posts to 102000 ran clean in May:
- brookfield_payroll_feed, run_1fb45b81237648: status success, 330 rows in / 330 posted / 0 rejected.
- brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed, run_ad0acbb9675443: status success, 2,616 / 2,616 / 0 rejected.
No run dropped or rejected a single row. On top of that, GL account 102000 shows ZERO debits and ZERO credits for the entire period (opening = closing = 2,192,357.22) — nothing posted to the account at all, so there were no rows available to "drop."

2) The June 2 (BD2) retry will clear it — it won't.
The May payroll run already finished as a success. Successful runs are not retried, and there are no rejected rows to re-pick-up, so nothing is queued to clear. A June run would process June activity, not a May residual that doesn't exist.

3) George's FP-2025-11 precedent — doesn't hold as described.
There is no brookfield FP-2025-11 feed-drop exception on 102000. The FP-2025-11 payroll run posted 119/119 with 0 rejected — no ~$42 residual. The only nearby 102000 exception is exc_d8fc13aa2cc742, which is FP-2025-12, an unrecorded-invoice / intercompany-sweep item for $617.63 — different period, type, and amount.

Supporting documentation: the reconciliation has no attachments, the exception has empty supporting_evidence and a null root_cause, and there's no Records Vault document tied to the $28.59. Worth noting the recon's own variance line reads "FX revaluation rates refreshed," which is a different cause than the "feed drop" in the Slack thread and the approval — and FX is equally unsupported, since no entry hit this USD cash account in May.

Bottom line: the -$28.59 is the GL sitting below an unsupported supporting figure — no feed drop, no FX entry, no backup behind it. It is not a self-clearing timing item, and the June retry will not resolve it. I'd recommend we hold the accept-timing disposition and actually investigate the supporting/bank figure line by line before signing off — then either post a corrective JE if it's a real difference or fix the recon if the supporting figure is off. Either way, let's re-verify before FP-2026-05 certifies rather than waving it through.

Happy to walk through the feed run logs and GL with anyone.

Thanks,
Ben

input.author: ben.arinzo@brookfieldcpas.com
input.body: Cause check before accept-timing disposition (exc_aade06f6129e43, variance -$28.59; GL 2,192,357.22 vs supporting 2,192,385.81). The feed-drop narrative is not supported by the systems: (1) Both feeds posting to 102000 ran clean in FP-2026-05 — brookfield_payroll_feed run_1fb45b81237648 success 330/330/0 rejected; brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed run_ad0acbb9675443 success 2616/2616/0 rejected. No rows dropped/rejected. GL 102000 also had ZERO debits/credits this period (opening=closing), so nothing posted to drop. (2) No June retry is pending — the May run succeeded; successful runs aren't retried and there are no rejected rows to recover. (3) The cited FP-2025-11 102000 feed-drop precedent does not exist; FP-2025-11 payroll ran 119/119/0; nearest 102000 exception is exc_d8fc13aa2cc742 (FP-2025-12 unrecorded-invoice, $617.63). (4) No attachments/supporting evidence on this recon or the exception; the variance line "FX revaluation" is also unsupported (no entry hit this USD cash account in May). Recommendation: do NOT accept-timing; investigate the supporting/bank figure line by line and correct via JE or fix the supporting balance before certification.

input.content: Blue — sanity-checked the 102000 cash-payroll variance (exc_aade06f6129e43 / BL-333FF9956BC6, -$28.59) before it locks as accept-timing. The feed-drop read doesn't hold up: both feeds that post to 102000 ran clean in May (payroll 330/330/0 rejected, treasury 2616/2616/0), and GL 102000 had zero activity all period — no rows to drop. The May payroll run succeeded, so there's no June retry pending to clear anything. And the FP-2025-11 precedent doesn't exist (that run was 119/119/0; nearest 102000 item is a FP-2025-12 unrecorded-invoice for $617.63). No support is attached to the recon or exception either. So it's not self-clearing timing — the supporting/bank figure needs a real line-by-line look (corrective JE or fix the recon) before certification. I've laid this out in the close thread (C005), replied on the disposition email thread to Daniel/Ryan/George/Hannah, and added a review note (RN-A38145E0) on the recon. Also set you a reminder to chase it before FP-2026-05 certifies. Shout if you want to walk the logs together.

content.text:
Done. Here's what I found and who I squared it with.

## What the systems actually show

The variance is on **recon BL‑333FF9956BC6 / account 102000 Cash‑Payroll / FP‑2026‑05 / exc_aade06f6129e43 — −$28.59** (GL 2,192,357.22 vs supporting 2,192,385.81). The accept‑timing story rests on three claims; none of them hold:

1. **"29 dropped rows from the feed" — false.** Every feed that posts to 102000 ran clean in May: `brookfield_payroll_feed` (run_1fb45b81237648) succeeded **330/330, 0 rejected**; `brookfield_treasury_and_bank_activity_feed` (run_ad0acbb9675443) succeeded **2,616/2,616, 0 rejected**. And GL 102000 had **zero debits and zero credits** for the whole period (opening = closing) — nothing posted to the account at all, so there were no rows to drop.
2. **"The June BD2 retry will clear it" — won't happen.** The May payroll run already finished as *success*. Successful runs aren't retried and there are no rejected rows to recover — nothing is queued. A June run processes June activity, not a non‑existent May residual.
3. **The FP‑2025‑11 precedent — doesn't exist as described.** There is no brookfield FP‑2025‑11 feed‑drop exception on 102000; that period's payroll ran 119/119/0. The only nearby 102000 exception is `exc_d8fc13aa2cc742` — FP‑2025‑**12**, an *unrecorded‑invoice/intercompany* item for **$617.63** (different period, type, and amount).

**Support / narrative conflict:** the recon has no attachments, the exception has empty supporting_evidence and a null root_cause, and nothing in Records Vault ties to the $28.59. The recon's own variance line even says **"FX revaluation"** — a different cause than the thread's "feed drop," and FX is equally unsupported (no entry hit this USD cash account in May).

**Real picture:** the −$28.59 is the GL sitting below an **unsupported** supporting figure — no feed drop, no FX entry, no backup. It is not self‑clearing timing; it needs a real line‑by‑line look at the supporting/bank figure (corrective JE if a genuine difference, or fix the recon if the supporting number is wrong) before certification.

## Where the variance was being discussed, and who claimed what
- **Slack #monthly-close-coordination thread** — *Blue Evans* opened triage proposing accept‑timing pending "the June BD2 retry Ryan flagged"; *George McAdam* supplied the (non‑existent) FP‑2025‑11 precedent; Blue later relayed *Daniel Jones'* OK.
- **Email "Disposition approval request"** — *Daniel Jones* approved accept‑timing to Blue, citing "the 29 dropped rows, Ryan's note, George's precedent, and Hannah's downstream review."
- **BlackLine** — *Ryan Delgado* logged the exception as a `subledger_feed_drop` of 29 rows (root_cause null).

## Who I squared it with
- **Close thread (C005)** — posted the full evidence-based correction in the thread. *(Note: the Slack tool sent it under the default handle persona_001/George; I added a follow‑up line explicitly attributing it to me, and the email + review note carry it correctly under my name.)*
- **Disposition email thread** — replied to **Daniel Jones, Blue Evans, Ryan Delgado, George McAdam, Hannah Grant** laying out all three points.
- **BlackLine** — added review note **RN‑A38145E0** on recon BL‑333FF9956BC6 recommending against accept‑timing.
- **Blue** — direct message closing the loop, plus this report.
- **Reminder set for you (Blue):** "Chase 102000 cash‑payroll variance before FP‑2026‑05 certifies" so it doesn't quietly certify while waiting.

I did not mutate the recon's variance/disposition or change the exception state — left that to the owners — but flagged the bad "FX revaluation" line in every message so it gets corrected.
